title: why the old qemu version?
body: latest podman desktop (1.15) vm is packing qemu 8.2.7 on mac ``` % podman run --rm --privileged --pid=host debian nsenter -t 1 -m -u -n -i qemu-aarch64-static --version qemu-aarch64 version 8.2.7 (qemu-8.2.7-1.fc40) ```` which according to https://www.qemu.org/download/ is not the latest version. why not 'always' package the latest available release version of qemu? it's not like old version doesn't have bugs, but likelihood of someone reporting those bugs, getting them fixed upstream are high. so it's always a good idea to package the latest version and point people to report qemu bugs in their gitlab repo issue tracker. right now, we need to wait additional months for podman to package the latest version feels a bit off. :(

comments.body:
They key is in "fc40", it is using Fedora 40 (via CoreOS) and the new version comes in Fedora 41:
https://repology.org/project/qemu/versions
ah right, fc42 has the latest 9.2 https://packages.fedoraproject.org/pkgs/qemu/qemu/. any chance podman switches to bleeding-edge fedora version then?

The [Podman Machine OS](https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os) follows [Fedora CoreOS](https://fedoraproject.org/coreos/), so it will get there eventually. Minor updates (8.2.8) will come earlier...

`FROM quay.io/fedora/fedora-coreos:stable`

https://fedoraproject.org/coreos/release-notes?stream=stable

https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/?packages=qemu

If you wanted updates sooner, you could build your own image or perhaps run a regular Fedora (Rawhide) VM directly?

You could also skip system qemu-user packages, and use qemu from a container (https://github.com/tonistiigi/binfmt).

We are still in the progress to fully build out the required automation in https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os

But yes we use whatever is in fedora coreos stable except out own packages which we will side load to get the latest podman in the image without waiting so long.

And the next podman release will include a new image build which will be based on f41.
FCOS 41 was released last week, so hopefully next podman release will pick up the latest qemu. 🤞

Given FCOS follows Fedora; and it doesnt look like qemu is bound for Fedora 41, I wouldnt hold out hope a change until we switch F42.  And we wont switch to F42 until FCOS which is usually something like 2 weeks behind the Fedora releases.

I dont believe Fedora 42 has gone to beta yet.  But I would imagine when it does, after some time FCOS's `next` stream would be switching to 42 (testing and stable not likely).  When it does, you could consider doing:

* a build of https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/blob/main/podman-image/Containerfile citing next instead of stable or testing
* `podman os apply` your newly built image
